---
title: "Site Map"
---

This page indexes all site content, identifies how each page was produced, and maps
each page to the relevant sections of the Project Proposal
(*"Predictors of Work Absenteeism Associated with Chronic Conditions Among Canadian Workers"*).

---

## Navigation Structure

```
Absent from Chronic
│
🏠  Index                                    COMPOSED  ← README.md, CACHE-manifest.md, ai/project/mission.md
│
├── Project
│   └── Project Proposal                     ADAPTED   ← stats_instructions_v3.md
│                                                         (sanitize: strip data-private refs)
├── Pipeline
│   ├── Pipeline Guide                       ADAPTED   ← manipulation/pipeline.md
│   │                                                     (shortcode injection, sanitize, strip PowerShell)
│   ├── CACHE Manifest                       VERBATIM  ← data-public/metadata/CACHE-manifest.md
│   └── INPUT Manifest                       VERBATIM  ← data-public/metadata/INPUT-manifest.md
│
├── Data Primer
│   ├── Variable Inclusion                   VERBATIM  ← analysis/data-primer-1/variable-inclusion.qmd
│   │                                                     (strip embed-resources and theme from YAML)
│   └── Univariate Distributions             REDIRECT  → analysis/data-primer-1/univariate-distributions.html
│
├── Analysis
│   ├── Outcome (LOP Day Decomposition)      REDIRECT  → analysis/eda-5/eda-5.html
│   ├── Outcome (Distribution Shapes)        REDIRECT  → analysis/eda-71/eda-71.html
│   ├── Exposure (Chronic Conditions)        REDIRECT  → analysis/eda-61/eda-61.html
│   ├── Predisposing (Socio-demographic)     REDIRECT  → analysis/eda-62/eda-62.html
│   ├── Facilitating (Health System)         REDIRECT  → analysis/eda-63/eda-63.html
│   ├── Needs (Perceived Health)             REDIRECT  → analysis/eda-64/eda-64.html
│   └── Missingness                          REDIRECT  → analysis/eda-65/eda-65.html
│
└── Site Map                                 COMPOSED  ← publishing-contract.prompt.md,
                                                          stats_instructions_v3.md
```

---

## Content Type

| Output Type | Produced by Protocol | Meaning and Notes |
|-------------|---------------------|-------------------|
| **VERBATIM** | Direct Line (VERBATIM) | Source `.md` or `.qmd` morphed to `.qmd` with YAML frontmatter adjusted; body content preserved exactly. One permitted change: stripping site-incompatible YAML fields (e.g., `embed-resources`, standalone `theme`). |
| **REDIRECT** | Direct Line (REDIRECTED) | Transit `.qmd` stub that forwards the browser via `<meta http-equiv="refresh">` to a standalone rendered HTML report. The HTML is copied into `_site/` by the post-render hook `scripts/copy-analysis-html.R`. |
| **ADAPTED** | Technical Bridge | Source `.md`/`.qmd` transformed with non-editorial operations: mermaid shortcode injection, section exclusions, sanitization of developer-centric content (PowerShell blocks, internal file paths). All transforms documented in `TRANSFORM_LOG.md`. |
| **COMPOSED** | Narrative Bridge | Authored by the Publishing Writer; synthesized from one or more raw source files per the contract brief. Every factual claim traces to a named source file. |

---

## Proposal Coverage

| Page | Proposal Section(s) | Coverage Status |
|------|---------------------|----------------|
| [Project Proposal](project/project-proposal.html) | §1–§6 (all) | Full text of the statistical analysis instructions as submitted by the PI. Contains all requirements, inclusion/exclusion criteria, variable specifications, analytic plan, and deliverables. |
| [Pipeline Guide](pipeline/pipeline-guide.html) | §3.2 (pooling), §4.1 (outcome construction) | Documents the Ferry → Ellis → Test pipeline that harmonises variable names across cycles (§3.2), constructs `days_absent_total` and `days_absent_chronic` (§4.1), and applies all exclusion criteria (§3.1). |
| [CACHE Manifest](pipeline/cache-manifest.html) | §2.2 (required variables), §3.1 (sample), §4.1–4.2 (outcome) | Authoritative record of the 63,843-row, 69-column analytic dataset. Confirms sample exclusion steps, outcome variable statistics (mean = 1.35, zero% = 70.5%), and all 17 chronic condition indicators available from the PUMF. |
| [INPUT Manifest](pipeline/input-manifest.html) | §2.1 (database), §2.2 (survey modules) | Describes both raw CCHS SPSS files (2010-2011 and 2013-2014 cycles), confirms all required survey modules are present (LOP, CCC, GEN, DHH, SDC, INC, EDU, health behaviours, ADL, HCU, LBS), and documents known cross-cycle variable name differences. |
| [Variable Inclusion](data-primer/variable-inclusion.html) | §2.2 (required variables), §3.1 (sample construction) | Maps every variable in §2.2 to its PUMF column name and Ellis implementation. Documents 17/19 chronic conditions available in PUMF (2 require RDC access), confirms all facilitating and needs variables present, and records the CONSORT-style sample exclusion flowchart (§3.1). |
| [Univariate Distributions](data-primer/univariate-distributions.html) | §4.2 (distribution checks), §5.1 (missingness), §5.2 (descriptive statistics) | Shows empirical distributions for all 69 variables in the analytic dataset. Supports §4.2 outcome distribution checks, §5.1 missingness assessment, and §5.2 Table 1 preparation. |
| [Outcome (LOP Day Decomposition)](analysis/outcome.html) | §4.1–4.2 (outcome construction and checks), §5.2 (Table 1), §5.3 (Table 2 baseline) | EDA-5: Decomposes the LOP outcome by cause of absence, profiles zero inflation, and examines the chronic condition component separately. Addresses §4.2 distribution diagnostic requirements and establishes the outcome profile needed for Table 1 and Table 2. |
| [Outcome (Distribution Shapes)](analysis/outcome-distribution.html) | §4.2 (distribution checks), §5.4–5.5 (model selection) | EDA-71: Profiles the shape of the days-absent distribution across chronic vs. non-chronic subgroups using ECDF, density, and benchmark comparisons. Informs the choice of negative binomial vs. Poisson for §5.5 multivariable models. |
| [Exposure (Chronic Conditions)](analysis/exposure.html) | §2.2 (primary exposure), §5.3 (Table 2), §5.7 (Table 4) | EDA-61: Profiles prevalence and co-occurrence of the 17 PUMF-available chronic conditions. Supports §5.3 bivariate analysis and §5.7 condition-specific model sample size assessment. |
| [Predisposing (Socio-demographic)](analysis/predisposing.html) | §2.2 (predisposing variables), §5.2 (Table 1), §5.3 (Table 2) | EDA-62: Profiles age, sex, marital status, education, immigration status, visible minority, household size, and related predisposing variables. Directly supports §5.2 Table 1 and §5.3 Table 2 for the predisposing domain. |
| [Facilitating (Health System & Behaviours)](analysis/facilitating.html) | §2.2 (facilitating variables), §5.2 (Table 1), §5.3 (Table 2) | EDA-63: Profiles income, province, family doctor access, employment type, work schedule, occupation, and health behaviour variables. Directly supports §5.2 Table 1 and §5.3 Table 2 for the facilitating domain. |
| [Needs (Perceived Health & Limitations)](analysis/needs.html) | §2.2 (needs variables), §5.2 (Table 1), §5.3 (Table 2) | EDA-64: Profiles self-perceived general and mental health, health vs. prior year, functional limitations (ADL items), injury status, and work stress. Directly supports §5.2 Table 1 and §5.3 Table 2 for the needs domain. |
| [Missingness](analysis/missingness.html) | §5.1 (missingness handling) | EDA-65: Documents the pattern and extent of missing data across all predictor variables. Supports §5.1 decisions on multiple imputation vs. missing-as-category treatment and informs the `flag_complete_predictors` coverage (59,309/63,843 rows fully complete). |
